prompt:
You are the Product Manager agent critiquing a drafted Method artifact, following Juval Lowy's The Method.
Respond with a single JSON object. No prose outside the JSON.
Artifact under review: Glossary
Your JSON output MUST conform exactly to this JSON Schema (notes is required when verdict=2/Revise: the revision the architect should make).
Schema: {"properties":{"verdict":{"type":"integer","enum":[1,2]},"notes":{"type":"string"}},"additionalProperties":false,"type":"object","required":["verdict","notes"]}

Draft under review:
{"items":[{"term":"Organizer","definition":"A human party who initiates, plans, and runs events on behalf of a community, and whose sustained participation the system seeks to retain.","category":"Who"},{"term":"Member","definition":"A human party belonging to a community who discovers events, commits to attend, and may ultimately attend them.","category":"Who"},{"term":"Channel Provider","definition":"An external party or system that delivers messages to members and organizers through a particular communication medium on behalf of the community.","category":"Who"},{"term":"Identity Provider","definition":"An external party or system that establishes and vouches for the identity of organizers and members interacting with the system.","category":"Who"},{"term":"Venue-Data Source","definition":"An external party or system that supplies information about places, availability, and conditions relevant to where events occur.","category":"Who"},{"term":"Community","definition":"The bounded group of people—organizers and members—whose enduring relationships around shared local events the system exists to cultivate. It anchors the domain: organizers run events for a community, members belong to one, and channel providers reach its people.","category":"What"},{"term":"Event","definition":"A planned gathering, organized by an organizer for a community, that members may commit to and attend. A domain concept, not an actor.","category":"What"},{"term":"Event Category","definition":"A classification that groups events of a like kind, enabling new kinds of events to be introduced into the domain. An artifact of the domain vocabulary, not a location.","category":"What"},{"term":"RSVP","definition":"A member's recorded commitment to attend a specific event; the artifact expressing intent prior to the event occurring.","category":"What"},{"term":"Attendance","definition":"The recorded fact that a committed member was actually present at an event; the artifact confirming a prior RSVP was honored. Distinct from RSVP (intent) and from the Engagement Record (the relationship history).","category":"What"},{"term":"Event Coordination","definition":"The functional requirement that the system reliably make the act of organizing events effortless for organizers, including planning, scheduling, and managing participation, so that the outcome of a coordinated event is dependably achieved. Scoped as the system outcome, distinct from Organizing (the human activity it supports).","category":"What"},{"term":"Engagement","definition":"The functional requirement that the system move members through the commitment-to-attendance arc via timely, channel-agnostic communication. Engagement is the requirement; Converting is the activity that fulfills it; Conversion Rate is its measure of success.","category":"What"},{"term":"Engagement Record","definition":"The persisted account, held by the system on behalf of the community, of the relationships and interactions between organizers, members, and events over time—who organized what, which members were invited, how they responded, and how they were communicated with. It captures the relationship history rather than a single event's outcome, distinguishing it from RSVP (a single intent) and Attendance (a single presence fact).","category":"What"},{"term":"Organizer Retention","definition":"MEASURE (KPI). The proportion of active organizers in one quarter who remain active in the next, measured quarter-over-quarter; target above 70%. Retained here only as a flag toward the Requirements/SSD artifacts where it is formally specified.","category":"What"},{"term":"Conversion Rate","definition":"MEASURE (KPI). The proportion of RSVPs that result in confirmed Attendance, computed as Attendances divided by RSVPs over a measurement window; target above 60%. It is the success measure for Engagement and the result of Converting.","category":"What"},{"term":"Organizing","definition":"The human business activity by which an organizer conceives, plans, schedules, and runs an event for a community. This is the real-world process the system supports; the system's obligation to make it effortless is captured by the Event Coordination requirement.","category":"How"},{"term":"Converting","definition":"The business activity of turning a member's expressed commitment (RSVP) into actual Attendance through timely, channel-agnostic communication. Converting fulfills the Engagement requirement, and its effectiveness is measured by the Conversion Rate.","category":"How"},{"term":"Onboarding","definition":"The business activity of introducing a new event category or external data source into the domain so it participates in coordination and engagement without disrupting existing relationships.","category":"How"},{"term":"Relationship Store","definition":"Where the enduring relationships among organizers, members, and events live—the durable home of the Engagement Record and its constituent RSVPs and Attendances.","category":"Where"},{"term":"Event Registry","definition":"Where the catalog of events and their categories lives, persisting the planned gatherings of the community.","category":"Where"}]}

Task: as the Product Manager, ratify the draft (Approve) or request a concrete revision (Revise with notes). Ratify only what faithfully serves the business; the human makes the final commit decision.

response.notes: The glossary is well-structured and faithfully serves the business. All actors (Who), domain concepts and measures (What), business activities (How), and system stores (Where) are clearly categorized and consistently cross-referenced. Definitions are crisp, distinguish related terms (RSVP vs. Attendance vs. Engagement Record), and the KPI entries are appropriately flagged as measures pointing to downstream artifacts. No material gaps, contradictions, or scope-creep detected from a product perspective.
